methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing samples for pollen analysis, including chemical digestion and mineral separation.

steps.description: 51 samples (2 cm³) were taken in the LGEH interval with a volumetric syringe at regular intervals of approximately 100 years once the age-depth model was known.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples at regular intervals using a volumetric syringe

steps.description: Samples were digested with NaOH for disaggregation, HCl for carbonate removal, and HF for silicate removal, and submitted to centrifugation with Thoulet solution (density 2 g/cm³) for mineral separation.
steps.searchable_summary: digest samples with NaOH, HCl, HF, and centrifuge with Thoulet solution

steps.description: Two tables of Lycopodium spores (Batch 280521 291; 13,761 ± 448 spores/tablet) were added to each sample before processing.

methods.description: Identification of pollen and spores using reference collections and databases.

steps.description: Pollen/spore identification was based on usual pollen catalogues for the region and the author's own reference collection.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen/spores using catalogues and reference collections

steps.description: NPP identification was based on previous research and the NPP Image Database.
steps.searchable_summary: identify NPP using previous research and NPP Image Database

methods.description: Counting pollen grains and performing statistical analysis to stabilize confidence intervals and saturation of diversity.

steps.description: Counting was conducted up to a minimum pollen sum of 300 grains, stabilization of confidence intervals and saturation of diversity.
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen grains to a minimum of 300 for statistical stability

steps.description: In samples where Pinus was superabundant, counts were stopped at 100-150 grains and extrapolated using the ratio between introduced and counted Lycopodium spores.
steps.searchable_summary: extrapolate Pinus counts using Lycopodium spore ratio

methods.description: Interpretation of paleoecological data using modern-analog studies and independent paleoclimatic proxies.

steps.description: Paleoecological interpretation was based on modern-analog studies for both pollen/spores and NPP.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret paleoecological data using modern-analog studies

steps.description: Paleoclimatic trends were inferred from pollen-independent proxies such as OST2 (temperature) and Seso (moisture) speleothem records.
steps.searchable_summary: infer paleoclimatic trends using independent proxies

methods.description: Recording and analyzing charcoal particles as fire proxies.

steps.description: Charcoal particles above 5 µm were recorded and used as fire proxies.
steps.searchable_summary: record charcoal particles above 5 µm as fire proxies

steps.description: Two size classes of these particles were considered, above and below 150 µm, as proxies for fires around the coring site and regional fires.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze charcoal particle size classes for fire proxies

methods.method_name: Pollen Diagram Zonation and Cluster Analysis
methods.description: Zonation of pollen diagrams and cluster analysis using statistical methods.

steps.description: Zonation of pollen diagrams was performed using the optimal splitting by information content (OSIC) and broken-stick methods.
steps.searchable_summary: perform pollen diagram zonation using OSIC and broken-stick methods

steps.description: Cluster analysis used the Pearson linear correlation coefficient (r) and the unweighted pair-group average (UPGMA) clustering algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis using Pearson correlation and UPGMA
